title: ED wound documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports laceration or abrasion, location, size and depth, bleeding, contamination, foreign-body concern, underlying structures, distal perfusion, dressing, splinting, tetanus, and return or disposition documentation.
red_flags.rationale: The exact WHO/ICRC open-wound section supports onset and mechanism context, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, clinician risk context, focused wound and distal-perfusion examination, appearance and existing procedure status only when assessed.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports laceration or abrasion, location, size and depth, bleeding, contamination, foreign-body concern, underlying structures, distal perfusion, dressing, splinting, tetanus, and return or disposition documentation.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured

red_flags.text: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.
red_flags.rationale: The exact SAMPLE history and disposition material supports patient concerns, measured vital signs, reviewed tests, clinician plan, discussion and arranged handover or follow-up without selecting wound closure, tetanus, antibiotics or disposition.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports signs and symptoms, allergies, medicines, past history, last intake, events or mechanism, examination, interventions, reassessment, and disposition documentation.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured

examination.text: Supports inspection for wounds, bruising, swelling, tenderness, deformity, range of movement, neurological findings, severe pain, and other injury signs.
examination.rationale: The trauma secondary survey was reviewed for injury context; only the generic manual focused-assessment fields were mapped.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports inspection for wounds, bruising, swelling, tenderness, deformity, range of movement, neurological findings, severe pain, and other injury signs.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured

source_limitations: Wound site, size, depth, contamination, foreign body, neurovascular and tendon status, bite or exposure, tetanus status, imaging, closure, procedure, medicine, dressing and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
